position_summary: Claude's plan is directionally honest, but it still overestimates what the proposed analyses can prove. The feasible local work can move the paper from tool demonstration to a better-calibrated evidence-integration manuscript, but it will not establish correctness at scale unless the claims are narrowed and the C3/C6 analyses are reported as diagnostics rather than validation or joint FDR.

findings.title: C3 is under-addressed: proposed baselines are mostly internal diagnostics, not external validation
findings.evidence: FEASIBLE but insufficient. Cross-condition artifacts exist at docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/live-fullsweep-loose/{sweep_Stim8hr.json,sweep_Rest.json,sweep_Stim48hr.json,all_conditions_top.json}; local data support condition-specific referee checks via data/*.suppl_table.csv and src/arbiter/lbd/referee_triple.py. But these are the same Perturb-seq substrate, not external ground truth. Literature/OpenTargets baselines are available via tools/ and src/arbiter/lbd/sources.py, but Open Targets is already used as an exclusion gate in src/arbiter/lbd/propose.py, so it is not an independent performance target.
findings.concern_path: If the revision presents cross-condition held-out or Open Targets enrichment as external validation, reviewers can still say the manuscript only re-applies the same substrate and hand-built priors. That fails the load-bearing C3 demand for whether supported calls are correct.
findings.suggested_action: Run C3 anyway, but label it precisely: (1) internal temporal robustness across Rest/Stim8hr/Stim48hr, (2) random/literature-rarity/OpenTargets comparator for prioritization behavior, not accuracy. Add a small literature-curated positive-control panel of canonical Th1/Th2 regulators and autoimmune-disease genes using PubMed/S2/OpenAlex tools if network/API access is available; otherwise call this a limitation, not solved validation.

findings.title: C6 'joint FDR' is statistically overclaimed; a permutation diagnostic is feasible, but not a true global null
findings.evidence: FEASIBLE as a bounded offline diagnostic from data/cluster_autoimmune_enrichment_results.suppl_table.csv, data/lbd_out/sweep_Stim8hr.json, data/lbd_cache/, and src/arbiter/lbd/propose.py. Existing Control 2 already permutes T3 disease labels and found observed 406 below null 467.7 +/- 10.9 in docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_results.json. INFEASIBLE as a defensible 'whole-pipeline joint FDR' unless one specifies a valid generative null for AB co-mentions, BC co-mentions, Open Targets known scores, A-universe construction, and T3 enrichment simultaneously.
findings.concern_path: Calling a disease-label shuffle 'joint FDR' invites a direct methodological objection: it preserves literature and gate structure while randomizing only one inherited substrate column. It answers 'is the T3 disease label assignment special under this shuffle?', not 'is 30/22,039 globally surprising under the whole discovery procedure'.
findings.suggested_action: Rename the C6 analysis to 'pipeline-level permutation diagnostic' or 'T3-label global-null diagnostic'. Report expected clean survivors under several constrained nulls: disease-label permutation within T3, gene-label permutation within condition, and eligible-pair randomization. Do not use FDR language unless a real multiple-testing correction family is defined.

findings.title: C4 cannot be resolved with current repo data; locus-wide neighbor check is useful but cannot discharge LD/colocalization
findings.evidence: PARTLY FEASIBLE. Existing evidence covers cis-expression and cluster co-membership: docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md states NAB2 is ~1.9 kb from STAT6, ac_lit=6, zero mechanistic papers, and no colocalization/LD control; docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/stage3/stage3_cis.json stores the STAT6 cis check; docs/replication_report_2026-07-08.md says corrected clusters 90/100 were re-derived and STAT6 was absent. NEEDS-FRESH-CS-RUN/LIT for a real 12q13 GWAS/coloc literature pull. INFEASIBLE from current CSVs alone because no LD, fine-mapping, variant, or colocalization data are present.
findings.concern_path: The plan risks implying that 'other 12q13 neighbors do not share the cluster' resolves an LD concern. It does not: the disease label can still be inherited from GWAS locus-level evidence even if perturbation clusters are genome-wide and STAT6 is not cis-repressed.
findings.suggested_action: Promote C4 to a foreground limitation. Run the locus-wide neighbor check as a negative-control sanity check, not a resolution. Add a literature subsection on 12q13 atopic dermatitis/asthma GWAS and state explicitly that colocalization is unavailable here; phrase NAB2 as a Perturb-seq-supported regulator of a STAT6-governed atopic axis, not an LD-resolved disease gene.

findings.title: C2 random out-of-funnel discrimination is feasible, but the planned metric needs a real comparator and should not be sold as 'confident no' accuracy
findings.evidence: FEASIBLE offline from data/DE_stats.suppl_table.csv, data/guide_kd_efficiency.suppl_table.csv, data/cluster_autoimmune_enrichment_results.suppl_table.csv, and src/arbiter/lbd/referee_triple.py. Existing anecdotal out-of-funnel ledger exists at docs/perturbseq-qc_2026-07-07/pyzobot_referee_results.md for EGR2/IL2/SLC1A5. Existing Control 1 in docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_results.json shows 2,430/2,430 failed-KD genes return untested, but that is near-tautological because the sample is defined by failing the KD gate.
findings.concern_path: A random panel that reports many QC-untested calls may only prove the gate encodes itself. A matched null must ask whether the referee makes different decisions for genes/pairs that a naive LBD or literature-only method would nominate, not arbitrary uninteresting genes.
findings.suggested_action: Use two panels: (1) arbitrary genes for calibration of untested/refuted labels, clearly descriptive; (2) hard negatives from literature-plausible or proposer-eligible pairs that fail exact-C/effect gates. Compare against naive literature-only nomination and report confusion-style buckets, not accuracy claims unless labels are independently known.

findings.title: C10 threshold sensitivity is feasible but not with the current sensitivity panel alone
findings.evidence: FEASIBLE with existing repo/cache for Stim8hr, but it requires extending code. Existing docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_panel.py only covers Control 1 QC, Control 2 label shuffle, and Control 3 beta/w/w2 rank stability. It does not sweep ab_gate_pct, min_bc, tau/ac_known, or alternative objectives. src/arbiter/lbd/propose.py exposes sweep(condition, min_bc, tau, ab_gate_pct, beta, w, w2). data/lbd_cache/ contains many cached Europe PMC/OpenTargets responses; data/lbd_out currently stores only Stim8hr outputs.
findings.concern_path: If the revision says threshold sensitivity was handled by the existing panel, reviewers can point out that the criticized gates were not swept. The current panel only addresses ranking weights after survival, not the survival decision itself.
findings.suggested_action: Add a small deterministic grid over ab_gate_pct, min_bc, and tau for Stim8hr, reporting clean survivor count, NAB2 survival/rank, and Jaccard overlap of supported sets. Keep it small; a full grid over all conditions is lower value than C3/C4/C6.

findings.title: C9 reconciliation is feasible but needs fresh literature result inspection, not just restating ac_lit=6
findings.evidence: PARTLY FEASIBLE. The tension is documented in docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md: ac_lit=6 but zero papers making the specific NAB2-Th1/Th2-eczema mechanistic claim. src/arbiter/lbd/sources.py computes Europe PMC hitCount with pageSize=1, so the cache may only preserve counts, not enough metadata for all six papers. tools/pubmed_fetch.py, tools/crossref_lookup.py, and tools/semantic_scholar.py exist for LIT work, but network is restricted in this session.
findings.concern_path: A reviewer will not accept 'raw co-mentions versus mechanistic papers' unless the six co-mentions are enumerated and classified. Otherwise the novelty claim still looks internally inconsistent.
findings.suggested_action: Run a targeted LIT audit listing the six Europe PMC co-mentions with title, year, context, and whether each asserts NAB2->Th1/Th2, NAB2->eczema, or merely name co-occurrence. If network is unavailable, mark this NEEDS-FRESH-CS-RUN/LIT rather than claiming it is done.

findings.title: The plan still needs a sharper hierarchy: C3/C4/C6 before extra figures and CS-workbench narrative
findings.evidence: FEASIBLE presentation fixes exist, but they are not load-bearing. Figure gap is real in the dossier; existing sensitivity outputs are at docs/manuscript/analysis/sensitivity_results.json; CS/browser artifacts are extensive under docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/. Reviewer concerns C13/C16/C17 explicitly say CS overreaches. Conversely, C3/C4/C6 are the evidentiary objections that decide whether the manuscript moves beyond 'machinery behaves as designed'.
findings.concern_path: Spending revision budget on Fig 5/self-audit and agentic reproducibility while C3 external validation, C4 LD, and C6 global-null remain weak will read as evasion. The genuine contribution is the receipt-backed falsification architecture, not the browser automation story.
findings.suggested_action: Minimum figure set: Fig 1 architecture with construction-vs-referee separation, Fig 3 or a compact ledger/funnel with C1/C2/C6 diagnostics, Fig 4 NAB2 chain with 12q13 caveat, and the sensitivity/null panel. Drop or supplement Fig 5 unless space remains. Keep CS in Methods/appendix as the reproducibility vehicle, not a co-equal result.

findings.title: Strategic answers: the honest reframe is necessary but not sufficient
findings.evidence: Repo-grounded answers to the five open questions: (1) reframe alone is not enough; C3 diagnostics plus a constrained C6 null are needed, but must be described as diagnostics. (2) C4 cannot be resolved from current files; docs/lbd_finding_nab2_2026-07-08.md already admits no LD/coloc, so foreground limitation. (3) CS should be Methods/appendix plus a short reproducibility note; docs/cs-full-pipeline_2026-07-09/ supports replicable execution but not epistemic validation. (4) essential figures are architecture, funnel/ledger+controls, NAB2 receipt+12q13 caveat, sensitivity/null panel; self-audit is optional. (5) highest value per effort: C4 LIT/limitation, C6 constrained null, C3 baseline/internal temporal robustness, C10 threshold grid, then C2 large random panel; all but true LIT/coloc can be run from local CSVs/cache, while C4 GWAS/coloc and C9 paper enumeration need fresh LIT/CS access.
findings.concern_path: Without this ordering, the revision can become a long defensive expansion that still leaves the decisive methodological criticisms untouched.
findings.suggested_action: Revise the plan order: first settle claims and limitations, then run C6/C3/C10, then build figures. Treat C2 random-panel as secondary unless it is designed around hard negatives. Do not claim wet-lab or LD validation; preserve the untested-vs-refuted and falsification thesis.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Which exact claim should the revised manuscript defend as its top-line result: calibrated receipt-backed prioritization with falsification diagnostics, or empirical correctness of supported calls? The analysis priority and wording differ substantially.
